Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use confirm channels and account for callback results, waitForConfirms, nack, return ordering, close, and unresolved publication state.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Confirm Channel Contract · changed case · Quiz Confirm Channel Contract
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz messaging team handles case 4746 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound the confirm window and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Retire confirmed entries, reject nacked entries, preserve ambiguous entries, cancel waiters, and prove zero memory or callback leaks across channel generations.

hints: For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Publisher Ledger Cleanup, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Publisher Ledger Cleanup precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Publisher Ledger Cleanup, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz messaging team handles case 4464 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound the confirm window and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Retire confirmed entries, reject nacked entries, preserve ambiguous entries, cancel waiters, and prove zero memory or callback leaks across channel generations.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Publisher Ledger Cleanup, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: The client library automatically retains a durable application ledger across reconnection.
